questionHtml:
Chlorine undergoes many reactions.

Outline why the chlorine atom has a smaller atomic radius than the sulfur atom.
markschemeHtml:
Cl has a greater nuclear charge/number of protons/Zeff «causing a stronger pull on the outer electrons» ✔

same number of shells
OR
same «outer» energy level
OR
similar shielding ✔

meta.title: Outline why the chlorine atom has a smaller atomic radius than the sulfur atom.
